# anti-slop-slop-canon

> An open-source Agent Skill that removes the writing habits that make text sound machine-made, then learns a transparent, editable profile of your voice. One profile serves both written and spoken content. It is free, MIT-licensed, and runs entirely as a local skill — no hosted model and no text sent to any service.

## What it does

- **Default cleanup, before any setup.** Out of the box it strips AI "tells": hype ("thrilled to announce", "game-changer"), ornamental punctuation (em-dashes, exclamation points, semicolons used for drama), and invented claims. Output stays plain, factual, and defensible line by line.
- **Learns your voice.** It builds a profile from a sample of your own writing, so cleaned text can be restated in your personal voice (e.g. clipped first person, dry understatement, a fixed sign-off) using only the facts present.
- **Written and spoken.** The same profile drives prose and a realtime voice prompt module.
- **Transparent and editable.** The voice profile is a plain, human-readable file you can inspect and edit.

## Onboarding

The first run offers three ways to start:
- **Personalize now** — provide writing samples and build a voice profile immediately.
- **Use the defaults** — get the baseline cleanup with no setup.
- **Defer** — start with defaults and personalize later.

## Install

- Global (all projects): install the skill into your user-level Claude Code skills directory.
- Project (this repo only): install the skill into the project's `.claude/skills` directory.

See the site and repository for exact commands.

## Links

- Website: https://useantislop.com
- Source (GitHub): https://github.com/codeSTACKr/anti-slop-slop-canon
- License: MIT

## Facts

- Type: Agent Skill for Claude Code (developer tool)
- Price: free ($0), open source
- Privacy: runs locally; the showcase site is static pre-rendered HTML with no server runtime and sends no text to any service.
